name: Praline French Toast- Super B'fast- Radio
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 Cups half and half OR skim milk
8 slices of French bread, 3/4" thick to cover the bottom
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, whisk together eggs, half and half, brown sugar, and vanilla until well combined.
2. Pour half of the mixture into a 9x13" pan.
3. Lay the French bread slices on top to cover the bottom of the pan.
4. Spread the remaining mixture over the bread, ensuring it is evenly covered.
5. Cover with foil and refrigerate overnight to allow the bread to soak up the mixture well.
6. In another 9x13" glass or Corningware pan (at the same time or the next morning), melt margarine in a saucepan over medium heat.
7. Add brown sugar, syrup, and pecans. Stir until combined.
8. Place the soaked bread into the nut mixture.
9. Cover with foil if baking the same day or use fresh if baking the morning of.
10. Bake at 350°F for 30-35 minutes, or until puffed and golden brown.
11. Serve by cutting each piece in half. Discard any burnt or overcooked pieces that may have caramelized on the bottom of the pan.
12. For a slight browning under the broiler (optional), place the pan under the broiler for 1-2 minutes, watching carefully to prevent burning.
